methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Bellatrix Aerospace is a pre-Series B stage space and aerospace company that has raised $8M to date. The valuation analysis is situated within a fragmented comparable universe: 5 public Space & Aerospace peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trade at a median price-to-sales multiple of 59.8x, while 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace represent the investment stage spectrum. However, the data set does not support narrow-stage or valuation-tier peer matching; across 23 same-sector private companies examined, fewer than three meet simultaneous stage and tier filters, rendering peer-median methodology inapplicable.

Public-comp anchoring is similarly constrained by the absence of disclosed revenue for the target. The Space & Aerospace public median of 59.8x P/S cannot be applied without Bellatrix's top-line figures, which private companies rarely disclose. This methodological gap is material given the sector's wide multiple range and early-stage revenue profile variability.

The analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range without either (1) Bellatrix's revenue or ARR figures, enabling public-comp scaling, or (2) recent funding rounds or secondary transactions among the identified private peer set. Key uncertainties center on revenue trajectory post-$8M raise and the company's positioning within the heterogeneous Space & Aerospace subsector (launch, infrastructure, or satellite operations). Further data gathering on financial metrics is required before quantitative valuation can proceed.
